qa_pairs.question: What was the purpose of the Cedar compute cluster shutdown from January 13 to January 21, 2025, and what action was required from users regarding their data?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar compute cluster was shut down from January 13 to January 21, 2025, for infrastructure renewal. During this period, the Cedar `/scratch` filesystem was migrated to new storage, and users were advised to immediately move any important data to their `/project`, `/nearline`, or `/home` directories. Cedar cloud services remained operational.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the full power shutdown of the Niagara cluster on November 25-26, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The full power shutdown of the Niagara cluster on November 25-26, 2024, was necessary for main panel upgrades in preparation for the setup of the Trillium cluster. All Niagara services, including the cluster and scheduler, were paused during this time.

qa_pairs.question: Which Cedar services were affected by the outage from November 7 to November 8, 2024, and which remained operational?
qa_pairs.answer: From November 7 to November 8, 2024, Cedar compute nodes were unavailable due to an outage. However, Cedar login nodes, storage, and cloud services remained fully operational and unaffected during this period.
